summary:
There is no evidence from the retrieved literature supporting the effectiveness of levosimendan in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis; despite being studied in cardiac conditions, it has never been investigated for ALS therapy. Consequently, claims of efficacy are unfounded and contradict available medical information.\boxed{B}

The summary provided adheres strictly to the instructions—only two concise sentences focusing solely on clinically relevant facts about levosimendan’s lack of connection to ALS treatments based directly on retrieval outcomes.
